title: Signature does not require digest in signed headers
description: server.rs inbox handler verifies: (1) the HTTP Signature is valid, and (2) the Digest header matches the body hash. But it never verifies that 'digest' is listed in the signature's headers= parameter. An attacker who intercepts a legitimately signed request can replace the body and Digest header while keeping the original signature valid, as long as the original signature only covered e.g. '(request-target) host date'. The parse_signature_header() function even provides a default headers list including 'digest', but an attacker can explicitly send headers="(request-target) host date" to exclude it. Fix: after parsing the signature, reject requests where the signed headers list does not include 'digest'.
